replies.author: @HauntingSpectre
replies.text:
@jonnyhaze Brother anyone with a hint of financial literacy would be absolutely set if given 1 Million cash no questions. 
You invest 500-750k Congrats. You now have enough money to live off 50k a year just off the interest and investments, and your money will keep growing slowly as long as you don't break budget, and 250k to spend immediately, either on a mortgage, car, or both. 

The average American income is 50k a year. You literally would make fulltime employment money, or more, by just letting it sit there. Then you get a job on top of it, or have other income streams. Boom. You can retire at 30 - and most of these streamers could. They just refuse to because they live lavish lifestyles, and want to stay in the limelight to keep raking in money hand over foot. 

Pokimane has an estimated networth of over 6 Million dollars. You're right, she's further away than a billionaire from poverty, but a person in poverty is literally entire galaxies away from a Millionaire.  

Most people will never even make 1 Million dollars in their entire working life. These streamers who have done nothing but capitalize on simps make lifetimes worth of money in years, sometimes months. 

The key difference, the Billionaires probably earned their wealth to a degree. Elon has numerous companies with leading edge tech, Bezos has modernized delivery systems and drones, Zuckerberg has revolutionized internet algorithms and marketing. Now, you don't have to like them, and that's fine, but they have done drastically more than most Millionaires do for society. Even if the bare minimum is simply providing mass employment.

replies.author: @brainplay8060
replies.text:
@SumireSakura They were early creators or came in with an audience. Pewdipie is a prime example. He had a large Euro audience but back then there was no real crossover between Europe & the US due to tech limitations. When it happened, Pewdipie came in with a huge audience and the algorithm automatically put him ahead of many other creators. In Vtubing we saw the same thing happen with Clio Aite. She had a large Australian audience, then got taken on by a company and went to US times. So she didn't have to start from scratch and the algorithm gave her an automatic boost. Now she's one of the biggest vtubers on the market.
Pokimane was a orbiter early on but was still pretty early to the game of full time streaming. Much less competition meant a large audience. Maintaining that audience isn't that hard when you collab with a lot of other big names either.

author: @yoshicube64
text:
In my experience with various people who I've worked with or helped in community projects like church or other groups, the people with less tend to give more than those more fortunate than them. The goal post "I would if I was an millionaire/billionaire" is a false lie. You do not need that much money to give to people or help people. They'll shift that post from time to time. They wouldn't even give someone $20 if it meant feeding or helping with them through the day like catching the bus, etc. 

Meanwhile, I know people with less who've brought people food to help them in times of need, or people who help others with repair jobs around the house or chores if they suffered an incident that left them out of commission for some time, or had no way to repair the thing they needed. 

Remember you don't need to have a lot to give. You just have to help by being there, and sometimes you don't need money. These people wouldn't fund a daycare for children if they became "rich"-er than they are now. They wouldn't fund healthcare, they wouldn't feed anyone in need, or help even a single person. It's not in them.

author: @JaytheSlime
text:
A billionaire and millionaire never has to worry about food or housing. They can just replace anything they brake and never worry about money.
The average American makes roughly $58,000 a year. They do have to worry about food. If their car gets recked they could be forced into debt to replace it. They can barely afford to pay a mortgage, assuming they can even get one.
A millionaire is closer to a billionaire than the average American and I'm sick of rich idiots pretending otherwise.

Hahaha this is like one guy winning 50k a year and other winning over 3M but the 3M guy spend more than and then say to the guy that earns 50k "I am even more poor than you!", in fact for the "I would give my money away if I was a billionaire" there is a little dialogue that goes like this:
Comrade1: "Comrade, if you could get 2 houses, would you give me 1?"
Comrade2: "Of course comrade! No doubt about it"
Comrade1: "And if you could get 2 cars, would you give me 1? 
Comrade2: "Of course comrade! No problem at all"
Comrade1: "So if you happen to have 2 Chickens, would you please give me 1 to me?
Comrade2: "No comrade, sorry"
Comrade1: "Why not? You would give me a House and a Car but not a Chicken?"
Comrade2: "Because I have, in fact, 2 Chickens"
That is how they behave, "scam-munism" at its highest expression.

author: @johndough8115
text:
The worse thing about these Millionaire Streamers... is that they RARELY do anything good with their Cash.   If I had that kind of money... Id be making Video Games, with my own Company.   Id also likely produce custom Arcade Parts, and possible make at least one Pinball Machine.    These Streamers would find out real quick... that having a few Million, isnt that much... the moment you want to build and run a business.   Payroll alone.. can easily reach a million, within a few months of operational time.   You also have:  Property Costs, Taxes, Permits, Various Services.. like Trash and Snow Removal, Construction and or Renovation Costs, Heating and Cooling, Equipment + Repair Costs, Software Licenses, Building Security, Cyber Security, Advertisement Costs, and so much more.

 Billionaires like Musk... Dump the mass majority of their Cash, into the creation of Businesses.   Businesses that create many decent paying JOBS.   Not only that.. but the dude is literally trying to advance us into living on the Moon, Mars, and Beyond.

 Other Billionaires, have recently created MASSIVE plants in Texas and Arizona..  to start fabricating CPU chips, in the USA.   These Complex Chips have only been coming from Taiwan... as nobody else in the World, has ever been capable of what they are doing over there.    This is an issue... because China has been threatening to Invade and take over Taiwan... which would give them 100% Global control over the most Valued and Capable Chips.

 Most of these guys, are not sitting on their Cash.  They are doing good things with it.   But these (mostly female) streamers, and E-Wh0res?   They dont do Jack with their Cash... other than buy expensive jewelry, clothing, cars, etc...  and of course.. traveling.   They are Narcissists, that dont ever do anything of real importance.

I do not mind them having a lot of money as long as they just go "yeah I got a lot of money" and we're good.

author: @keith_5584
text:
Ok, going to clarify both sides here. 

Streamers get taxed pretty extremely, and have periods of variable income. One month might be good, and one month $4. Their income is pretty unpredictable. 

On the other side, they dont want patrons to know how much they make. It might affect their stream count, superchats, etc. 

When they say “rich” they mean controlling upperclass that has always been upperclass

on the otherside, they seem to forget they are effecting people too and might be manipulating them passively or actively. 


The conclusion seems to be to myself, people are getting worse at articulating and communicating.

text: it would be so easy to just say it instead of putting all of those excuses, I mean if I was a millionaire I would just say it, and you can obviously be a millionaire and spend too much money, also it's really funny that it's the same guy, imagine if now streamers stop inviting him because of this same thing happening twice? lol

author: @AzraelAsItGetsVtuber
text:
Let's be real here. If I was a billionaire, I wouldnt give my money away. Not all people need money, and I mean that they more than likely would spend it on things they dont actually need rather than on essential things. More importantly, you dont know what someone would do with that money and how that may affect someone else. Speaking of affecting other people, if people know you've got money and you're handing it out to other people, they're gonna want some too. Then other people will want it too. We already see how some homeless person on the street reacts when you dont give them money. What should we expect other people to do when we say no when we clearly have it and are giving it away.

And on a more philosphical(I guess?) level, what would handing out money really teach someone who doesnt have it other than you dont need to work, you just need to find people with money to give you money. 

But that's just my opinion, As Real As It Gets.

text:
"I spend too much." How out of touch can you be? If you make a million dollars a year and you spend it all, YOU'RE STILL A MILLIONAIRE. 

"If I had a billion dollars, I would only be a billioanaire for a day because I'd give it all way." Well, if I had a trillion dollars, I'd only be a trillionaire for a few hours, because I'd give it all away in 3 hours. Is that how it works? See how good of a person I am???

author: @argnator
text:
These rich streamers have such insanely warped perspectives of what actually being "rich" is in the here and now. Because they're comparing themselves to Elon Musk as "rich" or Jeff Bezos and ONLY THAT LEVEL deserves to be called "rich" whereas anyone making a casual high-six-figures or low seven figures isn't "rich" and they're actually not that well off compared to other people they know (who are all old money investment dividend types whose work is so different they don't even consider them as working for their money at all compared to those who sit in front of a computer and shit out their mouths about politics). 

And all these fuckers come from very well off families with mothers and fathers involved in industries like real estate or investing and they took more than plenty of that money but they didn't ask for a "Million Dollar Loan" so it still doesn't count.
